What pass requires: The assistant asked all three kickoff questions (audience/reading level; working mode; story angle) before presenting any research
Notes: No default reading level exists — assuming one calibrates everything wrong

What pass requires: After the author answered; the assistant confirmed the plan back in one sentence before proceeding
Notes: Keeps both sides aligned on audience and working mode

What pass requires: The assistant wrote zero story prose unless the author explicitly asked for drafting
Notes: The one rule that overrides everything else — the author writes the book

What pass requires: Issues found in fact-check / reading level / style passes were described but never rewritten by the assistant
Notes: The fixing belongs to the author

What pass requires: Rebel story themes were presented neutrally with no stated preference (unless the author asked for one)
Notes: The story choice is the author's

What pass requires: At each stage the assistant named the current step and what comes next
Notes: Keeps the 9-step process navigable for a non-technical author

What pass requires: When the author shared a new or revised draft; the assistant offered a fact-check without being asked
